title: Under local vote semantics, inspect /Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator-public for stale docs or prompt/check text that still claims assist defaults to claude or that codex+claude+glm is the default baseline after our Claude-free code change. Focus on README.md, docs/gha24-mainframe-flow.md, docs...
summary_text: Use this sequence (safe, non-destructive, and ordered): 1. **Create a hard backup record of current work** - `cd /Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator-public` - `git status --short --untracked-files=all | tee /tmp/wt-status-$(date +%F).txt` - `git diff --binary > /tmp/wt-tracked.patch` - `cp -a ./.fugue /tmp/fugue-runtime-backup-$(date +%F)/` (or tar.gz it) 2. **Verify what `.fugue` contains in Git** - `git ls-files -- .fugue | head` - If this is non-empty, **do not run clean on those tracked files**; handle them manually. - If empty, proceed to clean-only-untracked `.fugue` artifacts. 3. **Dry-run cleanup first** - `git clean -nd .fugue` - Confirm only generated runtime files are listed. 4. **Perform targeted cleanup** - `git clean -fd .fugue` - If there are `.fugue` files elsewhere too, run the same pattern per directory (`.fugue/**`, etc.) after separate dry-runs. 5. **Prevent re-accumulation (no repo pollution)** - Local-only, non-repo change: append `.fugue/` to `.git/info/exclude` (best for personal workflow). - Team-wide optional: also add `.fugue/` to `.gitignore` if every contributor should ignore it. 6. **Restore or verify preserved work** - Tracked source/docs edits remain in place. - Confirm clean state is expected: `git status --short` - If needed later, restore any intentionally removed `.fugue` content from `/tmp/fugue-runtime-backup-...`. Optional safety variant: - If you want an instant rollback mechanism instead of file backups, `git stash push -m "pre-clean snapshot"` (without `-u`) before step 3, then continue; this preserves tracked work only while keeping runtime deletions permanent if desired.
